_README: PLACEHOLDER compound definitions. The notebook reads this file instead of hard-coding candidate compounds, so the public repository names none. Replace this file with the real definitions to reproduce the recorded run; those live in the restricted repository aistanbulresearch/Dual_Shield_Data at the same path. Override the location with the COMPOUND_CONFIG environment variable.

_schema.key: identifier used for JSON fields in agent prompts and in the parsing code
_schema.name: display name used in prose
_schema.stage: administration order in the sequential strategy (1 = first)
_schema.shield: which resistance layer this agent addresses
_schema.validation_block: per-agent block injected into the hypothesis/combination validation prompts
_schema.pk_block: per-agent pharmacokinetic block injected into the optimization prompt

combination_label: Compound A + Compound B + Compound C

compounds.name: Compound A
compounds.short_role: Breaks the outer shield, releasing the brake on NK cells
compounds.rationale_hint: How the HLA-E/NKG2A findings in the data support this agent
compounds.validation_block:
- Target: The HLA-E/NKG2A inhibitory axis (Outer Shield)
- Rationale: In the data, HLA-E expression is high in CSCs and PD-L1 is virtually absent. ADAM10 and ADAM17 are suppressed — HLA-E cannot be cleaved from the surface and remains membrane-bound. In LIANA the NK inhibitory interaction score is high.
- Clinical status: [redacted in the public configuration]
compounds.pk_block:
- Administration: [redacted in the public configuration]
- Half-life: [redacted]
- Metabolism: [redacted]
- Known dose: [redacted]
- Immune-related adverse event risk: [redacted]
- CYP interaction: [redacted]

compounds.name: Compound B
compounds.target: The vimentin intermediate filament network (Inner Shield) + CSC ALDH1 activity
compounds.short_role: Destroys the inner shield, triggering anoikis
compounds.rationale_hint: How the Vimentin/pEMT findings in the data support this agent
compounds.validation_block:
- Target: The vimentin intermediate filament network (Inner Shield) + CSC ALDH1 activity
- Rationale: In the data, VIM expression is very high in CSCs and CDH1 is completely lost. Cells locked in a partial EMT state depend on vimentin for mechanical resistance.
- Clinical status: [redacted in the public configuration]
compounds.pk_block:
- Bioavailability: [redacted in the public configuration]
- Half-life: [redacted]
- Metabolism: [redacted]
- CYP inhibition: [redacted]
- Known dose: [redacted]
- Hepatotoxicity risk: [redacted]

compounds.name: Compound C
compounds.target: The TGF-beta signaling pathway (the "master regulator" feeding both shields)
compounds.short_role: Shuts off the master signaling tap that feeds both shields and weakens the fibrotic barrier
compounds.rationale_hint: How the TGF-beta/stromal findings in the data support this agent
compounds.validation_block:
- Target: The TGF-beta signaling pathway (the "master regulator" feeding both shields)
- Rationale: TGF-beta both upregulates HLA-E expression (feeding the outer shield) and drives the EMT/Vimentin process (feeding the inner shield). It also activates the CAFs that form the fibrotic barrier.
- Clinical status: [redacted in the public configuration]
compounds.pk_block:
- Bioavailability: [redacted in the public configuration]
- Half-life: [redacted]
- Metabolism: [redacted]
- Known dose: [redacted]
- Cardiotoxicity risk: [redacted]
- Clinical data: [redacted]
compounds.pubmed_claim: Stage 1 TGF-beta receptor I inhibitor, clinical trials
compounds.pubmed_query: TGF-beta receptor I inhibitor clinical trial

ddi_notes: - The Stage 2 agent inhibits CYP3A4 -> plasma concentration of the Stage 1 agent (a CYP3A4 substrate) increases
- No pharmacokinetic interaction is expected with the Stage 3 agent (biologic agent)
ddi_management_summary: Management of the Stage 2 / Stage 1 CYP3A4 interaction
